title: MD
biography: Brian Druker is an American oncologist who developed imatinib (Gleevec), the first successful targeted cancer therapy, transforming chronic myeloid leukemia from a fatal disease into a manageable condition. He is director of the Knight Cancer Institute at Oregon Health & Science University.
aiSummary: Brian Druker is an oncology specialist with an H-index of 140 at Oregon Health & Science University. Developed imatinib (Gleevec), the first targeted cancer therapy for CML. Based in Portland, United States.
techniquesInvented: Targeted BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase inhibition for CML
education: University of California, San Diego School of Medicine

timeline.title: Began clinical trials of STI571 (imatinib)
timeline.description: Initiated first human trials of the BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase inhibitor

timeline.title: FDA approved imatinib (Gleevec) for CML
timeline.description: Gleevec received accelerated FDA approval, transforming CML treatment

timeline.title: Lasker Award
timeline.description: Received the Lasker-DeBakey Clinical Medical Research Award for targeted cancer therapy

timeline.title: Japan Prize
timeline.description: Awarded the Japan Prize for groundbreaking cancer research
